Resorbed and replaced by bone faster than another biocomposite*37–39 With a history of established clinical use, REGENESORB Material is associated with: No severe osteolysis21 Resorption and replacement by bone within 24 months21,36,37 Sustained mechanical stability21,36,37 REGENESORB Material 21 Arthrex Biocomposite Material 40 Percentage of anchors 0 20 40 60 80 100 Not visible Partially visible Visible Clearly visible 21 months 28 months 65%15% 20% Cal cium sulfate: W o rk s i n e a rl y h e a l i n g s t a g e s a t 4 – 1 2 w e e k s 31,32,35 Poly L-lactic-co-glycolic acid (PLGA): Comprised of natural products–lactic acid and glycolic acid 30,34 – to provide structural integrity ß-tricalcium phosphate (ß -TCP): Sustained bone formation for over 18 months 29,33 i FT+PT tears Smith+Nephew ADVANCED HEALING SOLUTIONS Histologic evaluation of biopsy specimens obtained after rotator cuff repair augmented with a highly porous collagen implant1 Arnoczky SP , Bishai SK, Schofield B, et al. Arthroscopy. 2017;33:278–283. Conclusion In a series of second-look biopsies, the REGENETEN Implant demonstrated rapid host cell ingrowth and collagen formation, leading to progressive maturation and functional loading of new tissue. Retrospective study to histologically evaluate host-tissue ingrowth, host-tissue maturation and host-implant biocompatibility in biopsies collected from 7 patients (FT tears, n=6; PT tears, n=1) between 5 weeks and 6 months after rotator cuff repair augmented with the REGENETEN◊ Bioinductive Implant • Rapid host cell ingrowth and early collagen formation at 5 weeks • Compared with week 5 samples, increased collagen formation, maturation and organisation on the surface of the implant at 3 months (Figure 1) • Implant no longer visible at 6 months, with new tendon-like tissue and highly oriented collagen indicative of functional loading (Figure 2) Collection of Evidence Next study →Previous studies → Overview Results Figure 1. Photomicrograph of implant surface at 3 months. Remnants of implant still present (→) (haematoxylin and eosin x 100) Figure 2. Photomicrograph of implant surface at 6 months. No evidence of implant remaining (haematoxylin and eosin x 100) → → → → → → → x100 100/uni03BCm Images included with permission from Dr Craig L. Van Kampen Conclusion The REGENETEN Implant was associated with reductions in tear size and significant increases in mean tendon thickness at 3 and 24 months. Smith+Nephew ADVANCED HEALING SOLUTIONS Collection of Evidence Evidence of healing of partial-thickness rotator cuff tears following arthroscopic augmentation with a collagen implant: a 2-year MRI follow-up2 Bokor DJ, Sonnabend D, Deady L, et al. Muscles Ligaments Tendons J. 2016;6:16–25. Prospective study to evaluate new tissue formation, tear progression, pain and function in 13 patients with PT tears who received the REGENETEN◊ Bioinductive Implant after subacromial decompression without repair Overview Results • Significant increase in mean tendon thickness, versus pre-operative values, at 3 and 24 months (both p<0.0001) • At 12 months, new tissue was indistinguishable from underlying tendon in 92% of patients (12/13) • At both 12 and 24 months, all patients with measurable pre-operative tear sizes showed a reduction in tear size of ≥1 grade (n=10; Figure), with complete tear healing in 70% (7/10) of these patients • Significant improvements versus pre-operative scores observed in clinical outcomes throughout 24-month follow-up period (CMS; CMS-pain; ASES total score; ASES pain score (p≤0.01) PT tears Post-operative (months since surgery) Number of patients 0 1 2 3 3 6 12 24 4 5 6 7 8 Pre-operative No tear Low grade tear Intermediate grade tear High grade tear Figure. Sequential improvement and healing of rotator cu/f_f.liga defects over 24 months (n=10) Smith+Nephew ADVANCED HEALING SOLUTIONS Healing of partial-thickness rotator cuff tears following arthroscopic augmentation with a highly-porous collagen implant: a 5-year clinical and MRI follow-up3 Bokor DJ, Sonnabend DH, Deady L, et al. Muscles Ligaments Tendons J. 2019;9:338–347. 5-year follow-up of a prospective, single-arm study, evaluating tendon integrity, tendon thickness, pain and function in patients with PT rotator cuff tears receiving the REGENETEN◊ Bioinductive Implant in lieu of standard repair. 11/13 enrolled patients were available for assessment at 5 years • 73% of patients (8/11) had no decline in tendon integrity between 2 and 5 years (Figure) • Mean tendon thickness significantly decreased between 2 and 5 years (5.9 vs 5.2mm; p=0.0012), but remained significantly greater than pre-operative values (4.3mm; p<0.0001) • Significant improvements from baseline in pain and function were sustained to 5 years (p≤0.01) and were not significantly
